In this lesson, I want to show you how easy it is to password protect a web page or post in WordPress. Now, like I said in our prior lesson, this is the method that I recommend you use to secure your digital content, whether it be videos, audios, software, anything digitally delivered. This is an easy, fast way to secure it when you're first launching your new funnel. Now I'm inside of WordPress and what you're about to see can be used for a post or a page. Now what you'll see if you're familiar with WordPress is this section over here that says publish. Now, right now, the visibility is set to public, which means that if somebody went to this page, they'd be able to view all of the content. But if we simply click on edit, you'll see that there is public and there's password protected and there's private. Well, to make this a password protected page, all we do is click this little radio button and then you enter password right here. Then you click okay, click update and that's all there is to it. Now this page is password protected. Now when somebody goes to view this page, what they'll see is a little blurb that says the content you're trying to view is password protected and there'll be a little field for them to enter the password. The key is that you take this password and you put it either on the thank you page that new customers come to after they purchase your product and or you include it in the thank you welcome email for new customers. So they know exactly what this password is. Again, start with this and then you can move on to the more expensive options.
